exhibits.description: A puddle so still it holds the whole sky — clouds, birds and all. The only mirror in the world you look down into to see up.
The famous one: a whole tree growing downward into the ground. At least, that is how it looks in the puddle — and the puddle is the exhibit.
The museum's working wing. Worms stranded on the pavement after the rain are carried gently back to the grass — every visitor is allowed to help.
The one gallery where touching the art is not just allowed but required. Boots recommended. The art changes with every jump.
Plain gray water wearing a shimmer of every color at once. Nobody at the museum can explain it, and nobody needs to.
The museum's greatest treasure: a puddle exactly the shape of a whale. You will want to see it before lunchtime — the sun has opinions.

editions.title: The Puddle Museum
editions.status_note: Manuscript complete (13 spreads) — illustration and publication are owner-gated.

blocker.owner_action: Decide the §5 illustration gate — the manuscript is complete (13 spreads); commissioning illustrations is a money-decision only the owner can make.
blocker.unblocks: Illustration, then publication of this edition — the same one decision unblocks all three languages.
